questions.question: What is the organisation of similar cells along with intercellular substances performing a specific function called?
questions.explanation: In multicellular animals, a group of similar cells alongwith intercellular substances perform a specific function. Such an organisation is called tissue.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: Which of the following organs in the human body consists of all the four basic types of tissues?
questions.explanation: For example, our heart consists of all the four types of tissues, i.e., epithelial, connective, muscular and neural.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: The word anatomy conventionally is used for the study of the morphology of which of the following?
questions.explanation: The word anatomy conventionally is used for the study of morphology of internal organs in the animals.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: What is the most common species of frog found in India?
questions.explanation: The most common species of frog found in India is Rana tigrina.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: Frogs do not have a constant body temperature. Such animals are referred to as:
questions.explanation: They do not have constant body temperature i.e., their body temperature varies with the temperature of the environment. Such animals are called cold blooded or poikilotherms.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: The protective coloration exhibited by frogs to hide from their enemies is known as:
questions.explanation: They have the ability to change the colour to hide them from their enemies (camouflage). This protective coloration is called mimicry.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: Where do frogs take shelter during peak summer to protect themselves from extreme heat?
questions.explanation: During this period they take shelter in deep burrows to protect them from extreme heat and cold. This is known as summer sleep (aestivation)...
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: Why is the skin of a frog always smooth and slippery?
options: Due to the presence of scales
Due to the presence of mucus
questions.explanation: The skin is smooth and slippery due to the presence of mucus.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: How does a frog intake water into its body?
options: It drinks water through its mouth
It absorbs water through its skin
It gets water exclusively from food
It inhales water vapour through lungs
questions.explanation: The frog never drinks water but absorb it through the skin.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: The body of a frog is divisible into which of the following regions?
questions.explanation: Body of a frog is divisible into head and trunk (Figure 7.1). A neck and tail are absent.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: Which structure protects the eyes of a frog while it is in water?
questions.explanation: Eyes are bulged and covered by a nictitating membrane that protects them while in water.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: Which organ in the frog receives sound signals on either side of the eyes?
questions.explanation: On either side of eyes a membranous tympanum (ear) receives sound signals.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: How many digits are present on the fore limbs and hind limbs of a frog, respectively?
questions.explanation: The hind limbs end in five digits and they are larger and muscular than fore limbs that end in four digits.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: Which feature of the frog's hind limbs helps it in swimming?
questions.explanation: Feet have webbed digits that help in swimming.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: Male frogs can be distinguished from female frogs by the presence of:
questions.explanation: Male frogs can be distinguished by the presence of sound producing vocal sacs and also a copulatory pad...
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: Where is the copulatory pad located in male frogs?
options: On the first digit of the fore limbs
questions.explanation: ...and also a copulatory pad on the first digit of the fore limbs which are absent in female frogs.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: Why is the alimentary canal of a frog relatively short?
options: Because frogs do not chew their food
Because frogs have a high metabolic rate
questions.explanation: The alimentary canal is short because frogs are carnivores and hence the length of intestine is reduced.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: Which organ in the frog secretes bile?
questions.explanation: Liver secretes bile that is stored in the gall bladder.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: What is the characteristic shape of a frog's tongue used for capturing prey?
questions.explanation: Food is captured by the bilobed tongue.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: Partially digested food that is passed from the stomach to the duodenum is called:
questions.explanation: Partially digested food called chyme is passed from stomach to the first part of the small intestine, the duodenum.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: Through which structure does the duodenum receive bile and pancreatic juices?
questions.explanation: The duodenum receives bile from gall bladder and pancreatic juices from the pancreas through a common bile duct.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: In the digestive system of a frog, what is the specific function of bile?
questions.explanation: Bile emulsifies fat and pancreatic juices digest carbohydrates and proteins.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: The numerous finger-like folds in the inner wall of the frog's intestine that absorb digested food are called:
questions.explanation: Digested food is absorbed by the numerous finger-like folds in the inner wall of intestine called villi and microvilli.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: When a frog is in water, which organ acts as the sole aquatic respiratory organ?
questions.explanation: In water, skin acts as aquatic respiratory organ (cutaneous respiration).
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: During aestivation and hibernation, gaseous exchange in a frog takes place through the:
questions.explanation: During aestivation and hibernation gaseous exchange takes place through skin.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: How many chambers does the heart of a frog have?
questions.explanation: It has three chambers, two atria and one ventricle...
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: What is the membrane that covers the heart of a frog called?
questions.explanation: ...and is covered by a membrane called pericardium.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: Which triangular structure joins the right atrium of the frog's heart and receives blood through the vena cava?
questions.explanation: A triangular structure called sinus venosus joins the right atrium. It receives blood through the major veins called vena cava.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: The ventricle of a frog's heart opens into a sac-like structure on the ventral side called the:
questions.explanation: The ventricle opens into a sac-like conus arteriosus on the ventral side of the heart.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: The special venous connection between the liver and the intestine in a frog is called the:
questions.explanation: Special venous connection between liver and intestine... is called hepatic portal system...
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: Which of the following statements is true regarding the red blood cells (RBCs) of a frog?
options: They are nucleated and contain haemoglobin.
They are the only cells in the lymph.
questions.explanation: RBC's are nucleated and contain red coloured pigment namely haemoglobin.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: How does the lymph of a frog differ from its blood?
options: Lymph lacks a few proteins and RBCs.
Lymph is pumped directly by the heart.
questions.explanation: The lymph is different from blood. It lacks few proteins and RBCs.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: What are the structural and functional units of the frog's kidney called?
questions.explanation: Each kidney is composed of several structural and functional units called uriniferous tubules or nephrons.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: In male frogs, the ureters emerge from the kidneys and act as the:
questions.explanation: Two ureters emerge from the kidneys in the male frogs. The ureters act as urinogenital duct which opens into the cloaca.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: How do the ureters and oviduct open into the cloaca in female frogs?
options: They open through a common urinogenital duct.
They do not open into the cloaca.
They open directly into the urinary bladder.
questions.explanation: In females the ureters and oviduct open seperately in the cloaca.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: What type of nitrogenous waste is primarily excreted by a frog?
questions.explanation: The frog excretes urea and thus is a ureotelic animal.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: How many pairs of cranial nerves arise from the brain of a frog?
questions.explanation: There are ten pairs of cranial nerves arising from the brain.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: Which of the following structures is NOT a part of the frog's forebrain?
questions.explanation: Forebrain includes olfactory lobes, paired cerebral hemispheres and unpaired diencephalon. The midbrain is characterised by a pair of optic lobes.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: The midbrain of a frog is specifically characterised by a pair of:
questions.explanation: The midbrain is characterised by a pair of optic lobes.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: The medulla oblongata passes out through the foramen magnum and continues into the:
questions.explanation: The medulla oblongata passes out through the foramen magnum and continues into spinal cord...
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: Which sense organs in the frog are well-organised structures rather than just cellular aggregations around nerve endings?
questions.explanation: Out of these, eyes and internal ears are well-organised structures and the rest are cellular aggregations around nerve endings.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: Which of the following describes the eyes of a frog?
options: They are compound eyes with multiple units.
They are simple eyes possessing only one unit.
They are located internally without any external visibility.
questions.explanation: These are simple eyes (possessing only one unit).
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: In the male frog, the testes are adhered to the upper part of the kidneys by a double fold of peritoneum called the:
questions.explanation: ...which are found adhered to the upper part of kidneys by a double fold of peritoneum called mesorchium.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: How many vasa efferentia arise from the testes of a male frog?
questions.explanation: Vasa efferentia are 10-12 in number that arise from testes.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: The vasa efferentia enter the kidneys on their side and open directly into:
questions.explanation: They enter the kidneys on their side and open into Bidder's canal.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: What is the functional relationship between the ovaries and the kidneys in a female frog?
options: Ovaries share a duct with the kidneys.
Ovaries are connected to the Bidder's canal in the kidney.
There is no functional connection with the kidneys.
Ovaries secrete hormones directly into the kidneys.
questions.explanation: The ovaries are situated near kidneys and there is no functional connection with kidneys.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: Approximately how many ova can a mature female frog lay at a time?
questions.explanation: A mature female can lay 2500 to 3000 ova at a time.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: What type of fertilisation takes place in frogs?
questions.explanation: Fertilisation is external and takes place in water.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: The larval stage involved in the development of a frog is called a:
questions.explanation: Development involves a larval stage called tadpole.
questions.subject: Biology

questions.question: According to the summary, which tissues are sheet-like, lining the body's surface, cavities, ducts, and tubes?
questions.explanation: Epithelia are sheet like tissues lining the body's surface and its cavities, ducts and tubes.
questions.subject: Biology
